title: Muretia lutea
taxonomy: Muretia lutea is classified within the family Aizoaceae, which is a group of succulent plants commonly known as the ice plant family. This classification places Muretia lutea alongside other related species, such as Lithops and Conophytum. Muretia lutea is further classified within the tribe Ruschieae, a sub-group of Aizoaceae characterized by their unique leaf and flower structures. The genus Muretia is a small group of succulent plants native to southern Africa, with Muretia lutea being one of the few species within this genus. Muretia lutea is an example of a highly specialized plant that has adapted to the arid conditions of its native region.

The plant's leaves and roots are believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, Muretia lutea is also used to treat gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions. intro: Muretia lutea is a plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as a yellow gentian or golden gentian. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Muretia lutea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
